sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The RIGHT action moved the black object with white dots to the right. The boundary shifted from col 18-26 to col 21-29. The white dots also moved accordingly.
Now I need to understand the goal. The black L-shaped object with white dots is movable with arrow keys. The right panel has a charcoal L-shape and a yellow L-shape. The goal is likely to move the black object to match/overlap with one of these shapes.
Let me check the current position and figure out where the target shapes are. I should also check if there's a "collision" or "matching" behavior when the black object overlaps with the charcoal or yellow shapes.
Let me first understand the layout:
- Sky blue divider is at cols 30-32
- The black object can move across panels
- Charcoal L-shape is around rows 15-23, cols 36-44
- Yellow L-shape is around rows 45-53, cols 51-59
The black object's current boundary is [[15, 21], [15, 29], [23, 29], [23, 27], [18, 27], [17, 26], [17, 21]]
So it's an L-shape with the horizontal bar at rows 15-17 and the vertical bar at cols 24-26 going down to row 23.
The charcoal object boundary: [[15, 36], [15, 44], [17, 44], [17, 39], [18, 38], [23, 38], [23, 36]]
This is also an L-shape: horizontal bar at rows 15-17, cols 36-44, then vertical bar going down.
The yellow object boundary: [[45, 51], [45, 59], [47, 59], [47, 54], [48, 53], [53, 53], [53, 51]]
This is also L-shaped but lower down.
Let me try to move the black object to overlap with the charcoal shape. The charcoal shape is at cols 36-44, rows 15-23. The black object is at cols 21-29, rows 15-23. I need to move it right to reach cols 36-44.
Let me first try moving it all the way to the charcoal shape and see what happens. But first, let me check if I can move it through the sky blue divider.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# The black object moved right by 3 columns. Let me check its current position and try to move it to the charcoal shape.
# Current black object boundary: [[15, 21], [15, 29], ...]
# Charcoal shape is at cols 36-44, rows 15-23
# Let me try moving it further right to reach the charcoal shape

# First let me check current state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'B' and n['pixels'] == 40:
        print("Black object boundary:", n['boundary'])
    if n['color'] == 'c':
        print("Charcoal object boundary:", n['boundary'])

# Try moving right several times to reach the charcoal shape
# The black object is at cols ~21-29, charcoal at cols ~36-44
# Need to move about 15 more columns right, which is about 5 RIGHT actions (each moves 3 cols)
result = action(['RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT'])
print("\nAfter 3x RIGHT:", result.get('board_changed'), result.get('level_completed'))

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'B' and n['pixels'] == 40:
        print("Black object boundary:", n['boundary'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
